original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.version: NICE guideline NG120; recommendations page accessed 2026-07-18
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England; international guideline requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, children and young people with acute cough associated with upper respiratory tract infection or acute bronchitis.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary care and community assessment, antimicrobial prescribing, reassessment and referral for acute cough.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting acute-cough assessment, expected course, antimicrobial stewardship, reassessment, referral and self-care context. It does not create an autonomous diagnosis, prescription, dose, or UAE pathway.

exact_sections.heading: 1.1 Managing acute cough; assessment and expected course
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting acute-cough course, likely upper respiratory infection or acute bronchitis context, and pneumonia or COVID-19 pathway distinction without autonomous diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Referral and seeking specialist advice
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of signs or symptoms suggesting serious illness and referral or specialist advice when indicated.

exact_sections.heading: Self-care and antimicrobial prescribing
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting self-care discussion and antimicrobial-prescribing rationale, without selecting a medicine or dose automatically.

title: Cough (acute): antimicrobial prescribing
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England; international guideline requiring UAE adaptation
population: Adults, children and young people with acute cough associated with upper respiratory tract infection or acute bronchitis.
intended_setting: Primary care and community assessment, antimicrobial prescribing, reassessment and referral for acute cough.
